customer_anxiety_profile: Efficiency-minded, financially cautious customer who uses electricity conservatively and asked about hidden costs unprompted. Not motivated by bill savings — his frame is curiosity about solar energy use and whether it fits his consumption pattern. Likely to disengage quickly if the closer leads with a savings pitch.
unresolved_concerns: Customer's actual motivation for solar was never explored — 'I just wanted to see how best one could be used' was volunteered but not developed
Hidden-costs anxiety signaled at 05:44 ('is there any collision cost') — customer is watching for financial surprises
Bill figure of $2.50/month is anomalous — likely a transcription artifact or miscommunication; actual bill amount is unknown and must be confirmed before any design work
Townhome HOA roof ownership was verbally confirmed as personal responsibility but not documented — closer should verify
sensitive_topics_raised: Customer is at work and took the call briefly — time sensitivity is real
Spouse is on title and named as a required decision-maker; spouse was not included in any scheduling
exact_promises_rep_made: Rep promised to send a text after the call
Rep asked customer to find the Dominion bill and send a photo of it
closer_should_know: This customer's motivation is NOT bill savings — he explicitly rejected that frame and said he's tight on consumption. The closer must open with curiosity about his energy use pattern, not a savings pitch. The $2.50 bill figure is almost certainly wrong and needs to be confirmed before any design conversation. The spouse must be on the call — she was named as the decision gate and was not included in any scheduling on the setter call. If the callback converts without the spouse present, the close is structurally vulnerable.
